title: Slith

{{Quote|Intelligent snakes. Well, why not?|Suz Tanwa|<ref name="PH">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20040722yavin1|series=Yavin: The Big Red One|text=Part 1: Giant}}</ref>}}
The '''Slith''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Snake/Legends|serpentine]] [[Species/Legends|species]] native to the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Yavin 13/Legends|Yavin 13]]. Nomadic creatures, they lived in patriarchal tribes of up to fifteen individuals and had little civilization and no permanent structures. The Slith were [[Poison/Legends|venomous]], preferring to paralyze their prey before eating them alive. They communicated with each other via a rudimentary [[Slithese|language]] consisting of noises produced by rubbing their scales on the ground. Slith had little contact with [[The galaxy/Legends|the wider galaxy]], remaining largely undiscovered until the reign of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. During the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], their moon was wiped of all life, leading to their [[Speciecide|extinction]].

==Biology and appearance==
The Slith were a [[Limb/Legends|limbless]], serpentine [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]], growing to a maximum of five [[Meter/Legends|meters]]. They were covered in jagged scales and were often patterned in stripes or spots. They had milky-white, translucent eyelids, through which they were able to see; this adaption protected their [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] from sand that was frequently whipped up by the strong winds on their desert [[Moon/Legends|moon]]. Slith were equipped with [[Poison/Legends|venomous]] fangs with which to hunt prey. Their venom paralyzed their victims, enabling the Slith [[Hunting/Legends|hunters]] to return live spoils to their tribe; Slith preferred to consume their prey live. Slith locomotion involved skimming over sand using a twisting action. Alternatively, the Slith could move sideways (known as sidewinding), and in this manner could attain greater speeds. This was particularly useful while hunting, allowing for intense bursts of speed to catch prey. It worked equally well to avoid hazards.<ref name="GG2" /> While lacking limbs, Slith were able to manipulate items using their coils and mouths, picking up rocks and [[Spear/Legends|spears]]. Slith also had forked tongues.<ref name="PH" />

The Slith were nocturnal creatures, hunting for small [[Lizard/Legends|lizards]] and other [[Reptile/Legends|reptiles]] during the cool nights. During the hot day, they sought refuge in the shade of [[Giant cactus|giant cacti]] or other convenient locations. The moon was known to reach [[Temperature/Legends|temperatures]] as high as fifteen [[Standard (temperature)|Galactic Standard degrees]], so in the hotter summer [[Standard month/Legends|months]], Slith burrowed deep into sand. This protected them from the worst of the heat while retaining heat for the nights, which could drop to near freezing.<ref name="GG2" />

==Society and culture==
[[File:Cacti.jpg|thumb|left|Slith sheltered under giant cacti.]]
The Slith were a nomadic species, creating no permanent dwellings on their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Yavin 13/Legends|Yavin 13]]. An alpha [[Gender/Legends|male]] led a small tribe of Slith, of no more than eight to fifteen individuals.<ref name="GG2" /> In addition to the alpha male, tribes consisted of a "lieutenant" male,<ref name="PH" /> several mating females with their [[Youngling/Legends|young]], and a couple of juvenile males. When these males came of age, they were exiled from the tribe to form a new family or wander alone. These tribes followed a patriarchal model, with the male responsible for guarding the community's traditions while the females did most of the actual work hunting. Juvenile males were also assigned to protect the young during these hunts. Among the females a Slith known as the [[First Mate (Slith)|First Mate]] had authority.<ref name="GG2" />

The Slith developed a grammatically simple language known as [[Slithese]], the sounds of which were produced by rubbing their rough scales over the ground. A number of simple signals could be produced, which conveyed different meanings. The language could be learned by non-Slith also;<ref name="GG2" /> a number of [[Gerb]]—the second [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] species on the moon—learned this skill as a self-defense mechanism.<ref name="PH" /> Slith lacked vocal cords, and so were unable to speak [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]], and while they were able to produce some vocal noises, the unavoidable consequence of [[Water/Legends|moisture]] evaporation discouraged this developing into an actual language.<ref name="GG2" />

The Slith had a number of complex communal ceremonies, which provided confirmation to offworlders of their intelligence.<ref name="AE">''[[Alien Encounters]]''</ref> These included praying for the [[Soul/Legends|spirits]] of [[Death/Legends|dead]] ancestors and to desert [[God/Legends|gods]] worshiped by the Slith. This task was carried out by female [[Shaman/Legends|shamans]].<ref name="GG2" />

==History==
[[File:Dresk_Kist.png|thumb|right|Dresk Kist, a female Slith Shaman]]
The Slith were still many centuries from developing industrialization at the time of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. They had little contact with [[The galaxy/Legends|the wider galaxy]], and negligible contact with the Gerb; however, on occasion the two did cooperate. On such occasions, [[twilight lizard]]s proved to be valuable commodities to trade with the Slith. The small reptiles were an excellent source of water and nourishment. Yavin 13 had long been thought to be devoid of intelligent life, until an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Scout/Legends|scouting]] expedition discovered both the Slith and the Gerb. At this time, there were approximately nineteen million Slith on the moon.<ref name="GG2" />

The [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] also had some contact with the Slith, sending some negotiators to the moon to open relations with both species; however, the expedition was sidetracked when the [[Fernandin Scouting Expedition]], which was also on the moon at the time, accidentally introduced a [[Yavin plague|plague]] that threatened to wipe out the Gerb. When the plague threatened to transfer to [[Human/Legends|Humans]], the Imperial presence in the [[Yavin system/Legends|system]] considered bombarding the moon to prevent further spread. Preventing this massacre of the native lifeforms became the Rebels' priority over and against contact with the Slith.<ref name="GG2" />

A tribe of Slith also came into contact with the renowned treasure hunter [[Suz Tanwa]]. The [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] was searching for an old family heirloom left by her ancestor [[Suvam Tan]]. Traveling to Yavin 13 in her [[Starship/Legends|starship]], the ''[[Reeko]]'', she located the burial site of an ancient pod left by her ancestor as a clue. However, in the site she began to dig, four Slith were resting. They quickly surrounded her, one finally wrapping her in his coils and delivering her to the mouth of a nearby Gerb burrow. The Gerb were able to retrieve the pod from within their burrow without Tanwa digging through their roof.<ref name="PH" />

The Slith continued to live on the moon into the time of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], and their existence was known to the [[Padawan/Legends|Jedi students]] on the neighboring moon of [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] in [[22 ABY/Legends|22 ABY]].<ref name="LW">''[[Junior Jedi Knights: Lyric's World]]''</ref> However, this peaceful existence was brought to an end during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War|Yuuzhan Vong invasion]]. As part of their efforts to crush the [[New Jedi Order]], the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Invasion of Yavin 4|invaded]] and conquered the [[Yavin system/Legends|Yavin system]]. In addition to razing the [[Jedi Praxeum|Jedi temple]] on Yavin 4, they wiped Yavin 13 and Yavin 8 clean of life—causing the extinction of the Slith.<ref name="GATOR">''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]''</ref>

==Slith in the galaxy==
One notable Slith during the Galactic Civil War was [[Dresk Kist]]. Both shaman and First Mate of her tribe, Kist also excelled as a hunter. She frequently went on solo hunts while the rest of her tribe slept. It was on one such excursion that Kist encountered a Rebel scout team that was exploring the moon for a suitable location for a base of operations.<ref name="GG2" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Slith first appeared in ''[[Galaxy Guide 2: Yavin and Bespin]]'', published by [[West End Games]] in [[1989]]. This information was later repeated in ''[[Alien Encounters]]'' in [[1998]], alongside an original illustration. Further information on the Slith was provided in ''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'' ([[2004]]) and ''[[Yavin: The Big Red One]]'' (2004), an installment in the [[Wizards of the Coast]] [[Planet Hoppers]] series. 

A Slith by the name of [[Sssiii]] also appeared in the article "[[Le facteur X]]" in the French [[roleplaying game]] magazine [[Casus Belli (magazine)|''Casus Belli'']] in 1998. However, the magazine never received [[Lucas Licensing]] approval, and thus is non-canon.
